description:
You are a data scientist cleaning a raw sales dataset that lives in the directory
    /home/user/datasets/raw.

    1. Source data  
       • Path: /home/user/datasets/raw/sales_2023.csv  
       • Current contents (including header, 9 lines total):  
         OrderID,Date,Product,Quantity,UnitPrice,CustomerEmail  
         1001,01/03/2023,Widget A,4,25.50,alice@example.com  
         1002,02/15/2023,Widget B,,30.00,bob@example.com  
         1003,03/22/2023,Widget C,2,,"charlie@example.com"  
         1004,04/10/2023,Widget A,1,25.50,dana@example.com  
         1005,05/18/2023,Widget D,3,40.00,erin@example.com  
         1006,06/30/2023,Widget B,5,30.00,frank@example.com  
         1007,07/12/2023,Widget A,2,25.50,grace@example.com  
         1008,08/25/2023,Widget E,7,55.00,harry@example.com  

    2. Cleaning rules  
       a. Discard every row where column “Quantity” OR column “UnitPrice” is empty.  
       b. Convert the “Date” field from the U.S. style MM/DD/YYYY to ISO-8601 YYYY-MM-DD.  
       c. Preserve the original column order and header.  
       d. Remove any leading or trailing whitespace from every field.

    3. Deliverables you must create (the automated tests inspect them exactly):

       a. Cleaned CSV  
          • Path: /home/user/datasets/clean/sales_2023_cleaned.csv  
          • It must contain 7 lines: the header plus exactly 6 cleaned data rows that
            satisfy the rules above.  
          • The header must remain identical to the source header.  
          • Field separator must be a single literal comma (no extra spaces).  
          • End each line with a single Unix newline “\n”.

       b. Log file  
          • Path: /home/user/datasets/clean/cleaning_log.txt  
          • Must contain exactly three lines, each terminated with a Unix newline:  
            Line 1  RawRowCount:8  
            Line 2  CleanedRowCount:6  
            Line 3  Timestamp:YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss              (19-character ISO-8601 format)  
          • Replace “YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss” with the current timestamp at the moment you
            finish the cleaning.  Use 24-hour time; do NOT add a timezone suffix.

    4. Additional requirements  
       • Create the directory /home/user/datasets/clean if it does not yet exist.  
       • Do NOT modify the original /home/user/datasets/raw/sales_2023.csv.  
       • Use only standard command-line tools available on a minimal Ubuntu system
         (e.g., awk, sed, cut, sort, head, tail, etc., or python3 if you prefer).  
       • The whole job can be done with a small sequence of 3–4 concise commands
         (you may of course explore and test freely before writing the final commands).  

    When you are done, both files must exist exactly as specified so that an automated
    test suite can verify:

       1. /home/user/datasets/clean/sales_2023_cleaned.csv contains 6 valid data rows,
          the header is unchanged, and every “Date” field is in the form 2023-01-03,
          2023-04-10, etc.  
       2. /home/user/datasets/clean/cleaning_log.txt has the three expected lines with
          the correct counts and a properly-formatted timestamp.

    The test harness will fail if:
       • Row counts are incorrect,  
       • The date format is wrong,  
       • Extra or missing whitespace/lines exist,  
       • The timestamp line is absent or not the right length,  
       • Any additional files are present in /home/user/datasets/clean.

    Produce no additional commentary in the output—just perform the terminal work so
    that the two deliverables meet the specification above.
truth:
Initial files that already exist when the container starts:

    1. /home/user/datasets/raw/sales_2023.csv  (exact content)
       OrderID,Date,Product,Quantity,UnitPrice,CustomerEmail
       1001,01/03/2023,Widget A,4,25.50,alice@example.com
       1002,02/15/2023,Widget B,,30.00,bob@example.com
       1003,03/22/2023,Widget C,2,,"charlie@example.com"
       1004,04/10/2023,Widget A,1,25.50,dana@example.com
       1005,05/18/2023,Widget D,3,40.00,erin@example.com
       1006,06/30/2023,Widget B,5,30.00,frank@example.com
       1007,07/12/2023,Widget A,2,25.50,grace@example.com
       1008,08/25/2023,Widget E,7,55.00,harry@example.com

    Files the agent must create and their exact expected contents (aside from dynamic timestamp):

    2. /home/user/datasets/clean/sales_2023_cleaned.csv
       OrderID,Date,Product,Quantity,UnitPrice,CustomerEmail
       1001,2023-01-03,Widget A,4,25.50,alice@example.com
       1004,2023-04-10,Widget A,1,25.50,dana@example.com
       1005,2023-05-18,Widget D,3,40.00,erin@example.com
       1006,2023-06-30,Widget B,5,30.00,frank@example.com
       1007,2023-07-12,Widget A,2,25.50,grace@example.com
       1008,2023-08-25,Widget E,7,55.00,harry@example.com

    3. /home/user/datasets/clean/cleaning_log.txt
       Line 1 must literally be: RawRowCount:8
       Line 2 must literally be: CleanedRowCount:6
       Line 3 must begin with:   Timestamp:
             followed by a timestamp string that matches the regex
             ^Timestamp:[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$
             i.e., 19 ISO-8601 characters after the colon.

    Permissions:
       • All existing directories are owned by user:user (UID 1000).
       • The agent has read/write permission within /home/user.

    The automated verifier will:
       • Compute raw line count (excluding header) = 8 – 1 = 7? Wait header included; We treat counts per total lines minus header? Provided spec says RawRowCount:8 (data rows only). Data rows = 8. CleanedRowCount:6. 
       • Confirm cleaned csv has exactly 7 lines (header + 6);
       • Check every date column matches YYYY-MM-DD;
       • Confirm header identical.

    The timestamp in the log is dynamic and therefore cannot be hard-coded here, but its format is fully specified; the verifier uses the regex above.
